description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a wooden rectangular cabinet and four slender black legs, evoking a mid-20th-century design. The front features a large convex glass screen, flanked on the right by two prominent rotary dials and a speaker grille below them. On top, a pair of thin metal V-shaped antennas extend upward from a black base. The back panel is boxy and protrudes outward, with visible ventilation slats and a set of control switches. The wood grain texture is visible on all sides, and the overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, typical of classic analog televisions.
description: A vintage wooden television with a convex glass screen, rotary dials, speaker grille, and V-shaped metal antennas on slender black legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and antennas.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood grain panel and the protruding back section.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the ventilation slats, control switches, and the back panel's boxy shape.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain wood grain panel and the protruding back section.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick wooden bezel, brown color, rectangular, with a classic mid-century style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the front right, additional switches on the back panel.
